# Where next {#sec-next}

## What we learned

The field's diagnosis of BabyView — that contrastive word learning fails because
egocentric speech and vision are only rarely about the same thing — is correct,
and it points to two levers that both work and are cheap:

1. **Filter to aligned moments.** A free, precomputed CLIP score concentrates the
   signal. The gain is robust (+12 points cross-child), it beats brute scale
   (137k filtered > 1.14M unfiltered), and it has a count-vs-quality optimum
   (~22k pairs) rather than "more is always better."
2. **Ground to objects, not scenes.** Cropping to the dominant detected object
   adds another 3–4 points and gives the best result overall.

Neither lever needs a bigger model or more compute — they change *what the model
learns from*. That is encouraging for the larger program: it suggests BabyView's
apparent failure is a signal-selection problem more than a scale problem.

## The obvious next moves

- **Combine the levers.** Crop-grounding on the 0.26-filtered set is untested and
  should stack; this is the immediate experiment.
- **Real referent selection.** Region grounding currently crops the *largest*
  detected object, a saliency proxy. The right object is the one being *referred
  to*. Adding cues that predict reference — egocentric centrality, the child's
  attention, caregiver gaze/pointing, or matching the detected class to a noun in
  the utterance — is the path from "dominant object" to "named object," and
  connects directly to the intent-prior idea from the symbolic modeling work.
- **Unfreeze, carefully.** The frozen probe is for iteration. Once a
  data/selection recipe wins here, the test is whether it also fixes end-to-end
  training — including the loss-divergence behavior the probe cannot see.
- **Better evaluation.** The CDI-detection 4AFC is fast and in-distribution but
  uses detections as ground truth. Wiring in an external anchor (Konkle objects,
  or DevBench) would make the numbers comparable across the literature.
- **Scale the aligned set.** Only ~2,969 videos currently have detections and
  only part of the corpus is scored; extending both enlarges the aligned pool and
  the region-grounding training set.

## Status

The prototype infrastructure — data prep, frozen-feature embedding, the two-tower
model, the 4AFC eval, region grounding, and a per-GPU experiment dispatcher —
is in [`src/`](https://github.com/mcfrank/vlm-headcam/tree/master/src) and runs
end-to-end on ccn2. Frame embeddings (~1.27M) and all run logs are cached on the
cluster; this book renders from committed aggregates, with human-subjects frames
regenerated locally.
